frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current input blends with recent past to promote perceptual stability

frameworks.name: visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: memory delay is the locus where past-present blending emerges

frameworks.name: adaptation
frameworks.one_line: repulsive bias dominates immediately after perception, opposite of attractive bias

core_question: Does serial dependence arise at the moment of perception or during post-perceptual working memory?
key_assumptions: temporal autocorrelation of natural scenes motivates a stability mechanism
spatial delayed-response task isolates perception vs memory contributions
behavioral bias indexes the underlying representational state
manipulations: delay between stimulus and response varied parametrically
trial-to-trial inducer-target relationships across the delay axis
key_findings: immediate responses show adaptation but no attractive serial dependence
attractive blending emerges only as the memory period lengthens
the attractive effect peaks around a six-second delay
relative strengths of adaptation and serial dependence vary widely across individuals
interpretations: serial dependence is a mnemonic rather than perceptual phenomenon
stability across views is implemented in working memory, not early perception
limitations_noted: large individual variability in adaptation vs serial-dependence balance
